FIX_Tags_Description.name: Account
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The investment **account number** or mnemonic that the order is being executed for, or the account to which a trade should be allocated. This tag typically follows standard firm-specific or regulatory formats.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Required when AllocTransType (71) is 'New', 'Cancel', or 'Replace' in Allocation messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AdvId
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A unique identifier assigned by the advising firm or order originator to the **advertisement** message. This tag is used for tracking and referencing subsequent updates or cancellations of the advertisement.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Applicable to Indication of Interest (IOI) messages (MsgType='6'), where it helps track the initial advertising event.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AdvRefID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: An optional reference ID used to link or cross-reference the current advertisement message with a **previous advertisement** (AdvId). Often used in 'Replace' or 'Cancel' Advertisement messages to identify the target of the modification.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AdvSide
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Indicates the side of the order/advertisement, such as **Buy** or **Sell**. In certain contexts like IOIs, it can represent a desire to engage in a cross or trade with both sides.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Common values include 'B' (Buy), 'S' (Sell), 'T' (Trade), 'X' (Cross).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AdvTransType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **transaction type** for the Advertisement message, typically indicating whether the advertisement is new, a cancellation, or a replacement of an existing advertisement.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'N' (New), 'C' (Cancel), 'R' (Replace).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AvgPx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **average price** of all shares or contracts of an order that have been executed to date. This is calculated dynamically in Execution Reports and remains constant for the life of the order.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This field is crucial for Execution Reports (MsgType='8') to communicate the average price for cumulative fills.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BeginSeqNo
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The starting sequence number of a range of messages requested during a **resynchronization process** (e.g., in a ResendRequest, MsgType='2'). It defines the lower bound of the message recovery window.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in sequence gap-filling and recovery messages. The number specified is the first sequence number the sender wishes to receive.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BeginString
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Identifies the **FIX protocol version** being used for the message. This tag is mandatory and is always the first tag in the message body. It specifies the major dialect of FIX being used.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Example values include 'FIX.4.2', 'FIX.4.4', or 'FIXT.1.1' for the transport layer.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BodyLength
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The byte count of the message starting with tag 35 (`MsgType`) up to, but not including, tag 10 (`CheckSum`). This field is used for message parsing and integrity checking by the receiving application.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Crucial for session layer integrity. A mismatch between the calculated length and this value results in a session-level Reject (MsgType='3').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CheckSum
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **three-digit, modulo 256 sum** of all bytes in the message excluding the CheckSum field itself. This field is the final tag of a FIX message and is used for message integrity at the session layer.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Must be three characters and padded with leading zeros (e.g., **007**). A CheckSum mismatch results in a session-level Reject (MsgType='3').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ClOrdID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique order identifier** chosen by the client/order originator for the purpose of communicating the order to the broker/exchange. It must be unique for each order and order replacement initiated by the client.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For a New Order Single (MsgType='D'), this is the primary client identifier. For an Order Cancel/Replace Request (MsgType='G'), the ClOrdID is the ID for the *new* replacement order, while OrigClOrdID (41) references the *original* order being replaced.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Commission
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The amount of **commission** incurred on a trade, typically specified per unit of quantity or as a fixed amount. Used in Execution Reports and Allocation Instructions.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: The CommType (13) field is required alongside this field to clarify how the commission value is expressed (e.g., in percent, per share/contract, or as a net amount).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CommType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **method used to calculate the commission** amount found in the Commission (12) field. It is a coded value indicating if the amount is absolute, per unit, or a percentage.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '1' (Per Share), '2' (Per Unit), '3' (Fixed Amount), '4' (Percentage), etc.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CumQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **total quantity (shares/contracts) of an order** that has been executed against to date. It represents the cumulative filled quantity reported across all Execution Reports for a single order.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: When the order is fully filled, CumQty (14) will equal the original OrderQty (38). Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Currency
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **currency** in which a quantity or amount field (e.g., Price, OrderQty) is expressed. It uses standard ISO 4217 currency codes (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'JPY').
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This currency applies to the instrument/order being described, contrasting with SettlCurrency (120) for settlement funds.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: EndSeqNo
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The ending sequence number of a range of messages requested during a **resynchronization process** (e.g., in a ResendRequest, MsgType='2'). It defines the upper bound of the message recovery window.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: If a Resend Request is issued with BeginSeqNo (7) and EndSeqNo (16) set to 0, it indicates a request for all available messages *after* BeginSeqNo.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExecID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique execution identifier** assigned by the broker/exchange to a single execution report. Each new fill, partial or complete, generates a new ExecID, even if they pertain to the same order.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Multiple execution reports for a single order will share the same OrderID (37), but each will have a unique ExecID (17) for the individual transaction/fill.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExecInst
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Detailed instructions to the broker or execution destination regarding the **handling and routing of an order**, beyond basic order type and time in force. This can include instructions for matching, display, or special terms.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This field supports multiple space-separated instructions, such as 'A' (All or None), 'O' (Open), 'T' (Trend following), etc.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExecRefID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The reference ID of an execution report that is being **canceled or corrected**. Used in Execution Report (MsgType='8') when the ExecTransType (20) is 'Cancel' or 'Correct'.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: When correcting or canceling a trade, the ExecRefID must match the ExecID (17) of the original Execution Report being modified.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExecTransType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the type of transaction represented by an Execution Report (MsgType='8'), indicating whether it's a **new trade, a correction, or a cancellation** of a previous trade.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (New), '1' (Cancel), '2' (Correct), '3' (Status) (Deprecated/Replaced).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: HandlInst
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Instructions for **automated versus manual order handling** at the broker or exchange. This is one of the initial, high-level routing instructions.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Common values include '1' (Automated execution order, private), '2' (Automated execution order, public), '3' (Manual order).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecurityIDSource
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Identifies the **scheme or authority** used to generate the security identifier found in SecurityID (48). This is critical for interpreting the SecurityID accurately.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Common values include '1' (CUSIP), '2' (SEDOL), '8' (Exchange Symbol), 'C' (ISIN).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOIID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The unique identifier assigned to the **Indication of Interest (IOI)** message (MsgType='6'). This tag is used for tracking and referencing subsequent updates or cancellations of the IOI.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in IOI messages (MsgType='6').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOIOthSvc
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Reserved for future use and typically **no longer utilized** in modern FIX versions. It was originally intended to specify other services related to an IOI.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Should typically be omitted in favor of newer, more explicit fields.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOIQltyInd
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A measure of the **quality or confidence level** associated with the quantity advertised in the IOI (e.g., how firm the interest is).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'H' (High), 'M' (Medium), 'L' (Low).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOIRefID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The reference ID of a previous IOI message that the current IOI is **replacing or canceling**. Used when AdvTransType (5) is 'C' or 'R'.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOIQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts)** of the security that the firm is advertising interest in. Can be expressed as a specific number or a coded value representing block sizes.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values can be numerical or coded, such as 'L' (Large) or 'M' (Medium).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOITransType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the type of transaction for the Indication of Interest (MsgType='6'), indicating whether it's a **new IOI, a cancellation, or a replacement**.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'N' (New), 'C' (Cancel), 'R' (Replace).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LastCapacity
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **capacity** in which the firm executed the last fill. This is required on Execution Report messages to meet regulatory and audit requirements, clarifying the firm's role in the transaction.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '1' (Agent), '2' (Principal), '3' (Riskless Principal). Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LastMkt
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **market or exchange** where the last fill was executed. Used to identify the venue of the trade, typically utilizing MIC (Market Identifier Code) values.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8') for trade venue reporting.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LastPx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **price** at which the last shares/contracts were executed (filled). For a single fill, this is the trade price. For a multi-fill execution report, this is the price of the latest fill being reported.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: A decimal quantity. Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8') and Market Data (MsgType='W').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LastQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts)** associated with the last fill being reported. This value represents the size of the most recent execution event reported in the message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For a trade to be fully reported, LastQty (32) and LastPx (31) must be provided in the Execution Report (MsgType='8').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoLinesOfText
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Indicates the number of lines of text that follow in the message body, specifically referring to the repeating group that contains the Text (58) field. Used mostly in administrative or generic message types.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Often used in Email messages (MsgType='C') to structure text content.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MsgSeqNum
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **message sequence number** that is incremented by one for each message sent by a specific SenderCompID (49). It must be unique and strictly increasing for the current FIX session.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is a mandatory header field. Sequence gaps trigger a Resend Request (MsgType='2').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MsgType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Defines the **purpose and format** of the FIX message itself. This coded value is crucial as it determines which fields are valid and mandatory within the message body.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is a mandatory header field. Examples: 'D' (New Order Single), 'A' (Logon), '8' (Execution Report), '3' (Reject).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NewSeqNo
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The next expected sequence number of the **counterparty** (TargetCompID) following a successful Sequence Reset (MsgType='4'). It allows a clean restart of the sequence count.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Sequence Reset messages (MsgType='4') for gap fill, or to reset sequences (ResetSeqNumFlag=Y).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrderID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **unique identifier for the order** assigned by the broker/exchange's order management system (OMS). This ID remains constant for the life of the order, even through modifications and fills.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This ID is returned in the Execution Report (MsgType='8') and is used by the client for subsequent Order Status Requests or Order Cancel/Replace Requests.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrderQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts)** originally specified by the client for a new order, or the revised quantity for an Order Cancel/Replace Request. It represents the *requested* total quantity.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is the 'live' quantity of the order. OrderQty minus CumQty (14) equals LeavesQty (151).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrdStatus
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **current status of the order** at the executing party (broker/exchange). It reflects the aggregate state of the order, including pending, active, partial fill, or final states.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Key values include '0' (New), '1' (Partially Filled), '2' (Filled), '4' (Canceled), '8' (Rejected). Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrdType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Defines the **order type**, which dictates how the order interacts with the market. This is a mandatory field for new orders.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Common values include '1' (Market), '2' (Limit), '3' (Stop). Used in New Order Single (MsgType='D').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrigClOrdID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **Client Order ID (ClOrdID)** of the original order message that is currently being referred to or acted upon. It is essential when submitting an Order Cancel Request or Order Cancel/Replace Request.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Key Note:** For cancel/replace operations (MsgType='G' or 'F'), ClOrdID (11) identifies the *current* request, while OrigClOrdID (41) points back to the *previous* order (the order being modified). This linkage is vital for the receiving system to correctly identify and process the modification against the existing order.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrigTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **time of the original message transmission** if the message being processed is a potential duplicate or resend. Used in conjunction with PossDupFlag (43).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss). Only required if PossDupFlag (43) is set to 'Y'.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: PossDupFlag
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator that specifies whether the message **may be a duplicate** transmission. This usually happens when the sending application believes the original message was not acknowledged and attempts to resend it.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Value 'Y' (Yes) indicates a potential duplicate. When 'Y', OrigSendingTime (122) must also be included to indicate the original transmission time.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Price
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **limit price** specified for a limit order (OrdType='2'), or the value used as a reference for other order types. This is generally the maximum price a buyer will pay or the minimum price a seller will accept.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: A decimal quantity. Not required for Market orders (OrdType='1').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RefSeqNum
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The sequence number of the message being **referenced or responded to**. Used in various response and session-level messages, such as ResendRequest (MsgType='2') or Reject (MsgType='3').
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For a Reject (MsgType='3'), this contains the MsgSeqNum (34) of the message that caused the rejection.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RelatdSym
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A symbol related to the security (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used in modern versions. Use the repeating group NoRelatedSym (146) for related instruments.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Rule80A
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A regulatory field (formerly known as Rule 80A) used to specify the **capacity or classification** under which the order is placed (e.g., individual, proprietary, agency).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Replaced by OrderCapacity (528) and CustOrderCapacity (582) in FIX.4.4 and later.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecurityID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **identifier of the security** being traded. This identifier is explicitly defined by the identifier scheme used (SecurityIDSource, 22).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Always used in conjunction with SecurityIDSource (22) to ensure the ID is correctly interpreted (e.g., CUSIP, ISIN, Bloomberg, etc.).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SenderCompID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **unique identifier of the company or entity sending the message**. This is critical for identifying the source system on the FIX session.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is a mandatory header field. It forms one half of the unique FIX session pair (SenderCompID, TargetCompID).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SenderSubID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **further breakdown or sub-identifier** of the SenderCompID, typically identifying a specific branch, desk, application, or individual within the sending company.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used to route messages within a firm. While optional, it's often used for internal message segregation.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SendingDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The date the message was sent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. All date/time information is typically derived from SendingTime (52).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SendingTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date and time of message transmission**. This is generated by the sender immediately before transmitting the message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss). This is a mandatory header field used for sequence management and latency checks.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Quantity
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A generic field used to express the quantity of a trade, order, or allocation in **shares or contracts**. Its context depends entirely on the message type.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Renamed to 'OrderQty' (38) and 'AllocQty' (80) in more specific contexts.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Side
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **side of the order** or trade, indicating the investor's intention (e.g., to buy or sell the security). This is a foundational field for all trading messages.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '1' (Buy), '2' (Sell), '5' (Sell Short). Mandatory for order-related messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Symbol
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **trading symbol** (ticker) of the security being bought or sold. While SecurityID (48) is preferred for unique identification, Symbol is often used for human readability and routing.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Usually supplemented or replaced by SecurityID (48) and SecurityIDSource (22).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TargetCompID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **unique identifier of the company or entity expected to receive the message**. This is critical for session routing and identification.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is a mandatory header field. It forms one half of the unique FIX session pair (SenderCompID, TargetCompID).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TargetSubID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **further breakdown or sub-identifier** of the TargetCompID, identifying a specific branch, desk, application, or individual within the receiving company.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used to route messages to a specific application or desk within the receiving firm.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Text
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **free-form text field** intended for human-readable comments, additional information, or error descriptions. It is usually placed near the end of the message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For encoded characters (e.g., non-ASCII), the EncodedText (355) and EncodedTextLen (354) pair should be used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TimeInForce
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **validity period** for which an order remains active. This determines when the order will expire if not fully executed.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Common values include '0' (Day), '1' (Good Till Cancel, GTC), '3' (Immediate or Cancel, IOC), '6' (Good Till Date, GTD - requires ExpireDate 432 and/or ExpireTime 126).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TransactTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **time of the event** for which the message is intended. For orders, this is the time the client transmits the order; for trades, it's the time of execution. This field represents the business event time.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss). This is the key timestamp used for business logic, contrasting with SendingTime (52), which is purely for session protocol timing.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Urgency
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Indicates the level of **execution urgency** associated with an order, which can affect routing and prioritization.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (Normal), '1' (Flash), '2' (Background).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ValidUntilTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The date and time until which a **quote or indication of interest (IOI) is valid**. After this time, the quote/IOI should be considered expired.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss). Used primarily in Quote (MsgType='S') and IOI (MsgType='6') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Indicates the **method or period of settlement**. This defines the delivery cycle for the security.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (Regular/T+3), '1' (Cash), '6' (Future - requires SettlDate 64).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **settlement date** for the transaction. This date dictates when the transfer of funds and security ownership will occur.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**. Required when SettlType (63) is 'Future' or other non-standard settlement cycles.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SymbolSfx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **suffix to the Symbol (55)**, used to uniquely identify a security or trading status where the base symbol is ambiguous (e.g., rights, warrants).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ListID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique identifier for an order list (or basket)**. This ID is used to manage and track the collective status of multiple orders submitted as a single list operation.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in List Order (MsgType='E') and List Status (MsgType='N') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ListSeqNo
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The sequential number identifying the **position of an order within a specific list (basket)**. Starts from 1 for the first order in the list.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in List Order (MsgType='E') messages to order the sequence of execution.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TotNoOrders
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **total number of individual orders** that comprise a list (basket) or are being submitted/referenced. This helps the receiver prepare for the incoming volume of individual orders.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in List Order (MsgType='E') and Allocation Instruction (MsgType='J') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ListExecInst
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Special **execution instructions applicable to the entire list of orders**. This dictates the coordinated handling of all orders within the basket.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Often contains instructions about whether to execute the entire list simultaneously (All-or-None) or sequentially.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **unique identifier** assigned to an Allocation Instruction message (MsgType='J') or Allocation Report message (MsgType='C'). This ID is used for tracking and referencing the allocation details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is the primary identifier for allocation messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocTransType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **type of transaction** for the allocation message, indicating whether it's a new allocation, a cancellation, or a replacement.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (New), '1' (Replace), '2' (Cancel), '3' (Preliminary).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RefAllocID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The AllocID (70) of a **previous Allocation Instruction** message that the current message is referring to, typically for replacement or cancellation.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used when AllocTransType (71) is '1' (Replace) or '2' (Cancel).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoOrders
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of individual orders** being referenced within the Allocation Instruction, representing the beginning of a repeating group for order details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Required in Allocation Instruction messages (MsgType='J') to identify the list of orders being allocated against.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AvgPxPrecision
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of decimal places** to be used when calculating and displaying the AvgPx (6). This is used to control precision in environments that demand fixed-point arithmetic.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TradeDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date the trade or execution occurred**. This is a historical date, distinguishing it from the settlement date.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**. Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8') and Allocation Instructions (MsgType='J').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExecBroker
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **broker or executing firm's identifier** that handled the execution of the trade. This is often used when a firm routes an order through another broker.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Allocation Instructions (MsgType='J') and Trade Capture Reports.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: PositionEffect
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Used for **options, futures, and other derivatives** to indicate whether the transaction is opening a new position or closing an existing one.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'O' (Open), 'C' (Close), 'F' (FIFO) - for US Options, and 'D' (Default).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoAllocs
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of separate allocations** being made within the message (i.e., the number of accounts receiving shares/contracts). This marks the start of the repeating group for allocation details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Mandatory in Allocation Instruction messages (MsgType='J') and Execution Reports (MsgType='8') when reporting an allocated trade.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocAccount
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **account number** or mnemonic to which the specified quantity (AllocQty, 80) is being allocated. This is the recipient account for the executed shares/contracts.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoAllocs (78).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts)** being allocated to the specific AllocAccount (79). The sum of all AllocQty fields should equal the total executed quantity of the trade.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoAllocs (78).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ProcessCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A field originally intended to specify **how a particular trade should be processed** (e.g., T+2 or T+3).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Its usage is often replaced by more specific fields like SettlType (63) and is sometimes deprecated or reserved for custom usage.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoRpts
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of individual executions/reports** related to a message, typically used in administrative or bulk messages to define the size of a repeating group.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Rarely used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8'), where NoExecs (124) is preferred.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RptSeq
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **sequence number of a specific report** within a series of reports. Used to ensure that reports related to a single event (like a trade) are transmitted and processed in the correct order.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CxlQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts) being canceled** in an Order Cancel Request (MsgType='F') or Order Cancel/Replace Request (MsgType='G'). This field specifies the reduction amount.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: In an Order Cancel Request (MsgType='F'), this is usually the full remaining quantity, resulting in a zero LeavesQty (151).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoDlvyInst
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of delivery instruction groups** following, indicating the start of a repeating group for delivery details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Settlement Instructions (MsgType='T') and Allocation messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: DlvyInst
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specific delivery instructions (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Use the nested repeating group defined by NoDlvyInst (85) and the Party-related fields (448, 452) for modern settlement instructions.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocStatus
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **status of the allocation process**. Used in Allocation Reports to communicate the final status of the requested allocation split.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (Accepted), '1' (Block Level Reject), '2' (Account Level Reject), '5' (Received).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocRejCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **reason why an Allocation Instruction was rejected**. This field provides a detailed explanation for the failure, categorized by FIX standard codes.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Only populated if AllocStatus (87) indicates a rejection ('1' or '2').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Signature
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **digital signature** for message authentication and integrity, often used for regulatory or audit purposes. The calculation method is complex and requires specialized handling.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Requires SignatureLength (93) immediately preceding it. Rarely used in standard FIX sessions due to performance overhead.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecureDataLen
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **length in bytes** of the SecureData (91) field immediately following it. Used for binary data management.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Required if SecureData (91) is present.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecureData
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A block of **encrypted or secured binary data**. The method of encryption is specified by EncryptMethod (98).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Rarely used for standard trading messages; more common for secure administrative or key exchange messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BrokerOfCredit
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The firm to be **credited** for the trade (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='10' (Broker of Credit) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SignatureLength
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **length in bytes** of the Signature (89) field immediately following it. Used for binary data management.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Required if Signature (89) is present.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: EmailType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **classification of the email message**, indicating whether it is an urgent alert, a regular document, or a news release.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Email messages (MsgType='C').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RawDataLength
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **length in bytes** of the RawData (96) field immediately following it. Used for binary data management.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Required if RawData (96) is present.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RawData
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A block of **unformatted binary data**, intended for proprietary or non-FIX-standard content that needs to be transmitted within the FIX message structure.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: The content must be preceded by RawDataLength (95).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: PossResend
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator that specifies whether the message is being **re-sent as a result of a Resend Request** (MsgType='2'). It indicates a gap fill message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Value 'Y' (Yes) indicates a possible resend (gap fill). Unlike PossDupFlag (43), this field indicates an *intentional* resend of missed sequence numbers.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: EncryptMethod
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **algorithm or method used for encryption** of the data block(s) within the message (e.g., SecureData, 91).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (None), '1' (PKCS), '2' (DES), etc.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StopPx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **trigger price** for a stop order (OrdType='3') or stop limit order (OrdType='4'). When the market price reaches or passes this price, the order is then released to the market as a market or limit order.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Required for Stop and Stop Limit orders.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExDestination
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **intended final execution venue** for the order or quote (e.g., the specific exchange, ECN, or dark pool).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: It is often a MIC (Market Identifier Code) value, specifying the physical or electronic trading destination.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CxlRejReason
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **coded reason** for rejecting an Order Cancel, Order Cancel/Replace, or Order Status Request message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (Too late to cancel), '1' (Unknown order), '2' (Broker/exchange option). Used in Order Cancel Reject (MsgType='9').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrdRejReason
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **coded reason** for rejecting a New Order Single or other order initiation message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (Broker/exchange option), '1' (Unknown security), '3' (Quantity exceeds maximum). Used in Execution Report (MsgType='8') when OrdStatus (39) is '8' (Rejected).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOIQualifier
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Provides further **qualification or restriction** regarding the Indication of Interest (IOI).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'A' (All or None), 'C' (At the close), 'I' (In touch with primary market).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: WaveNo
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Wave number associated with an order (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Should be omitted in modern FIX versions.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Issuer
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **name of the issuing company** for the security. Provides a human-readable reference for the security's source.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in security definition messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecurityDesc
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A free-form text **description of the security**.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For encoded characters, the EncodedSecurityDesc (351) and EncodedSecurityDescLen (350) pair should be used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: HeartBtInt
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **maximum time interval (in seconds)** allowed between sending any two messages without sending a heartbeat message. It monitors the health of the FIX session connection.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is a mandatory field in the Logon (MsgType='A') message. A lack of message traffic after this interval necessitates sending a Heartbeat (MsgType='0').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ClientID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The firm's internal **client/customer account identifier** (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='3' (Client ID) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MinQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **minimum quantity (shares/contracts)** required for an execution (fill) to take place on this order. If specified, the trade must be for at least this quantity.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used to create Minimum Quantity or 'MinQty' orders, typically preventing small, undesirable partial fills.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MaxFloor
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **maximum quantity** (shares/contracts) that is permitted to be displayed to the market at any given time. Used for 'Iceberg' orders, where only a portion is visible.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: If MaxFloor=0, the order is fully displayed. If MaxFloor is less than OrderQty (38), it is an iceberg order.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TestReqID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique identifier** assigned to a Test Request (MsgType='1') message. This ID is returned in the resulting Heartbeat (MsgType='0') message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used to force a Heartbeat response from the counterparty to verify the session link is operational and monitor network latency.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ReportToExch
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator that specifies whether the resulting trade **should be reported to the exchange** or regulatory authority.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'Y' (Yes) or 'N' (No). Critical for regulatory reporting and trade capture messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LocateReqd
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator required for **short sale orders**. If 'Y', the order sender warrants that the security has been located (borrowed) prior to sending the order.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values are 'Y' (Yes) or 'N' (No). Required for Sell Short (Side='5') or Sell Short Exempt (Side='6') orders.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OnBehalfOfCompID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **CompID of the firm for whom the message is being sent**. Used in 'third-party' or intermediary scenarios where the actual sender is a service bureau or hub.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is a standard header field when using message routing intermediaries.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OnBehalfOfSubID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **SubID of the party for whom the message is being sent**, further defining the entity identified by OnBehalfOfCompID (115).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in conjunction with OnBehalfOfCompID (115).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: QuoteID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique identifier** assigned to the quote message (MsgType='S'). Used for tracking and referencing subsequent quote status or cancellation requests.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: The primary identifier for a Quote message.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NetMoney
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **net monetary amount** for a trade or position, typically calculated as (Quantity * Price) minus commissions and taxes, or adjusted for settlement amounts.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Execution Reports and Allocation messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlCurrAmt
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **net amount in the settlement currency** (SettlCurrency, 120). This is the amount of funds that will actually move for settlement.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Required when the trade currency (Currency, 15) differs from the settlement currency.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlCurrency
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **currency in which the trade will settle**. Uses standard ISO 4217 currency codes.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Required when the settlement currency differs from the trade currency (Currency, 15).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ForexReq
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator specifying whether a **foreign exchange (FX) trade is implicitly requested** or required to cover the funding necessary for the settlement of the primary trade.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Value 'Y' (Yes) indicates an FX transaction is required for settlement funding.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrigSendingTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **SendingTime (52) of the original transmission of a message** that is now being resent (gap fill or duplicate). Used to correctly process messages when sequence numbers are reused or messages are delayed.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss). Mandatory when either PossDupFlag (43) or GapFillFlag (123) is 'Y'.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: GapFillFlag
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator, when 'Y', indicates that the message is being sent solely to **fill a sequence number gap** (MsgType='4', Sequence Reset) and contains no new business-level information.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used exclusively in Sequence Reset (MsgType='4'). It suppresses application processing on the receiving end; the receiver should only update their sequence number.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoExecs
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of individual executions (fills)** reported within an Execution Report (MsgType='8'). This marks the beginning of a repeating group for execution details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8') to signal a list of reported execution events.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CxlType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The type of cancellation (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used in modern FIX versions. The type of cancellation is implied by the message (e.g., Order Cancel Request, Mass Cancel Request).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExpireTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date and time when the Time In Force (59) expires** for the order. This applies when TimeInForce is 'GTD' (Good Till Date) or other time-bound instructions.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss). Required if TimeInForce (59) is '6' (GTD).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: DKReason
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **reason why a counterparty is sending a 'Don't Know' (DK)** trade acknowledgement message (MsgType='Q'), indicating they do not recognize the trade or the details are incorrect.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Don't Know Trade (MsgType='Q').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: DeliverToCompID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **CompID of a secondary destination** that the message should be routed to, typically an ultimate recipient beyond the immediate FIX session counterparty.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used for 'alternate' routing or multi-hop messaging.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: DeliverToSubID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **SubID of a secondary destination** that the message should be routed to, used in conjunction with DeliverToCompID (128).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used for 'alternate' routing or multi-hop messaging.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IOINaturalFlag
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator, when 'Y', signifies that the size of the Indication of Interest (IOIQty, 27) represents a **natural, committed, or firm interest** and not just an estimate.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values are 'Y' (Yes) or 'N' (No). Used in IOI messages (MsgType='6').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: QuoteReqID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique identifier** assigned to a Quote Request (MsgType='R') message by the initiating party. Used for tracking the request and linking it to subsequent quote responses.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: The primary identifier for a Quote Request message.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BidPx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **bid price** (the price at which a buyer is willing to buy).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Market Data Incremental Refresh (MsgType='X') and Quote (MsgType='S') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OfferPx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **offer price** (the price at which a seller is willing to sell).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Market Data Incremental Refresh (MsgType='X') and Quote (MsgType='S') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BidSize
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts)** associated with the BidPx (132).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Market Data Incremental Refresh (MsgType='X') and Quote (MsgType='S') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OfferSize
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts)** associated with the OfferPx (133).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Market Data Incremental Refresh (MsgType='X') and Quote (MsgType='S') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoMiscFees
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of miscellaneous fees** being reported in the message. This marks the beginning of the repeating group for fee details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Allocation Instruction (MsgType='J') and Execution Report (MsgType='8') messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MiscFeeAmt
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **amount of a specific miscellaneous fee** being reported (e.g., regulatory fee, tax, or other charges).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoMiscFees (136).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MiscFeeCurr
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **currency** in which the MiscFeeAmt (137) is expressed. Uses standard ISO 4217 currency codes.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoMiscFees (136).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MiscFeeType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **type or classification of the miscellaneous fee** being reported (e.g., Regulatory Fee, Tax).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoMiscFees (136).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: PrevClosePx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **previous day's closing price** for the security. Used for informational purposes or as a basis for price validation rules.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in market data and security status messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ResetSeqNumFlag
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator that instructs the counterparty to **permanently reset their incoming MsgSeqNum (34)** count. Used when sequences are permanently out of sync or at the start of a new trading day.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Key Note:** Set to 'Y' in Sequence Reset (MsgType='4') messages for a full reset. Requires NewSeqNo (36) to indicate the starting point of the new sequence.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SenderLocationID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **physical or logical location** identifier of the system transmitting the message (SenderCompID).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used for detailed audit trail and regulatory reporting.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TargetLocationID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **physical or logical location** identifier of the system intended to receive the message (TargetCompID).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used for detailed audit trail and routing.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OnBehalfOfLocationID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **physical or logical location** identifier of the party for whom the message is being sent (OnBehalfOfCompID).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in third-party or intermediary routing scenarios.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: DeliverToLocationID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **physical or logical location** identifier of the alternate destination (DeliverToCompID).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used for 'alternate' routing or multi-hop messaging.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoRelatedSym
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of repeating securities/symbols** referenced within the message. This marks the beginning of the repeating group for instrument details.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in complex messages like Market Data Request (MsgType='V') or Security List (MsgType='y') to list the securities involved.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Subject
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **subject line** of an Email message (MsgType='C').
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For encoded characters, the EncodedSubject (357) and EncodedSubjectLen (356) pair should be used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Headline
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **headline or title** of a News message (MsgType='B').
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For encoded characters, the EncodedHeadline (359) and EncodedHeadlineLen (358) pair should be used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: URLLink
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **URL reference** contained within a News message (MsgType='B') that points to the full text or source of the news item.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Must be a valid URL string.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExecType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Describes the **type of reaction/event** reported in an Execution Report (MsgType='8'). This specifies the reason the Execution Report was generated.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Key Note:** This often matches OrdStatus (39) but indicates the *action*. Values like 'C' (Canceled), 'F' (Fill), or 'I' (Order Status) trigger logic on the receiving side.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LeavesQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **quantity (shares/contracts) remaining** to be executed (unfilled) for an order. It represents the active quantity of the order available in the market.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Key Note:** Calculated as OrderQty (38) - CumQty (14). When LeavesQty = 0, the order is considered fully filled or canceled/expired.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashOrderQty
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The total quantity of an order **expressed as a cash value**. This is used when the security being traded is a fixed-income instrument or a fund.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used for orders specified by monetary amount rather than physical quantity.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocAvgPx
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **average price** used specifically for the allocation calculation.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Allocation Instruction messages (MsgType='J').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocNetMoney
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **net money** (quantity * price - fees) calculated specifically for the allocation.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Allocation Instruction messages (MsgType='J').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlCurrFxRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **Foreign Exchange (FX) rate** used to convert the trade currency (Currency, 15) to the settlement currency (SettlCurrency, 120).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in Execution Reports and Allocation messages when an FX conversion is necessary.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlCurrFxRateCalc
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **method used to apply the FX rate (155)** for conversion (Multiply or Divide).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values are 'M' (Multiply) or 'D' (Divide).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NumDaysInterest
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of days** that the accrued interest calculation is based on.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used primarily for fixed income securities.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AccruedInterestRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **rate (as a percentage)** used to calculate the accrued interest for the security.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity (e.g., 0.05 for 5%). Used primarily for fixed income securities.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AccruedInterestAmt
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **monetary amount** of accrued interest that the buyer must pay to the seller. This is included in the Gross Trade Amount.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used primarily for fixed income securities.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlInstMode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **mode of settlement instructions**, indicating whether default instructions apply or if specific details are being provided within the message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (Default), '1' (Specific Instruction).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocText
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A free-form text field providing **additional comments or information** pertinent to the allocation.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: For encoded characters, the EncodedAllocText (361) and EncodedAllocTextLen (360) pair should be used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlInstID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **unique identifier** assigned to a Settlement Instruction (MsgType='T') message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: The primary identifier for settlement instruction messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlInstTransType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **type of transaction** for the settlement instruction (e.g., New, Cancel, Replace).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include 'N' (New), 'C' (Cancel), 'R' (Replace).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: EmailThreadID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A field used in Email messages (MsgType='C') to **link related messages** together, forming an email conversation thread.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlInstSource
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **source of the settlement instructions**, indicating whether they originate from the investor, the broker, or a third-party service.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '1' (Broker's Instructions), '2' (Client's Instructions), '3' (Third Party).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlLocation
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Physical location of settlement (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='17' (Settlement Location) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecurityType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **category or type of the security** being described (e.g., Equity, Option, Future). This helps in defining the subsequent required fields for the instrument.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Common values include 'CS' (Common Stock), 'OPT' (Option), 'FUT' (Future).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: EffectiveTime
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date and time when the terms or instructions** contained in the message become effective or valid.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **UTC** (YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StandInstDbType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **type of standard instruction database** used by the counterparty (e.g., specific vendor or internal system).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StandInstDbName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **name of the standard instruction database** being referenced, used in conjunction with StandInstDbType (169).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StandInstDbID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **ID within the standard instruction database** that holds the settlement details.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlDeliveryType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the type of **security delivery** required for settlement (e.g., FOP-Free of Payment, DVP-Delivery Versus Payment).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values are '0' (Versus Payment) or '1' (Free).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlDepositoryCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The code for the settlement depository (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='19' (Settlement Depository) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlBrkrCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The settlement broker code (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='20' (Settlement Broker) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlInstCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The settlement instruction code (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by SettlInstRefID (214).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecuritySettlAgentName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The name of the security settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='11' (Security Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecuritySettlAgentCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The code of the security settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='11' (Security Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecuritySettlAgentAcctNum
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The account number of the security settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='11' (Security Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecuritySettlAgentAcctName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The account name of the security settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='11' (Security Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecuritySettlAgentContactName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The contact name of the security settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='11' (Security Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecuritySettlAgentContactPhone
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The contact phone of the security settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='11' (Security Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashSettlAgentName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The name of the cash settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='12' (Cash Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashSettlAgentCode
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The code of the cash settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='12' (Cash Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashSettlAgentAcctNum
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The account number of the cash settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='12' (Cash Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashSettlAgentAcctName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The account name of the cash settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='12' (Cash Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashSettlAgentContactName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The contact name of the cash settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='12' (Cash Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CashSettlAgentContactPhone
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The contact phone of the cash settlement agent (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/Replaced by PartyID (448) with PartyRole (452)='12' (Cash Settlement Agent) in the NoPartyIDs repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BidSpotRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The foreign exchange **spot rate** for the bid side of a currency pair. Used for FX instruments or trades where foreign currency conversion is involved.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Quote messages (MsgType='S').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BidForwardPoints
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **forward points** to be added to the spot rate to derive the forward FX rate for the bid side.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Quote messages (MsgType='S').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OfferSpotRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The foreign exchange **spot rate** for the offer side of a currency pair.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Quote messages (MsgType='S').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OfferForwardPoints
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **forward points** to be added to the spot rate to derive the forward FX rate for the offer side.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Quote messages (MsgType='S').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OrderQty2
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Secondary order quantity (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Orders with multiple quantities should use the structure of a Cross Order or Multi-leg order.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlDate2
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Secondary settlement date (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Multi-settlement date scenarios should utilize the NoDates (580) repeating group.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LastSpotRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The foreign exchange **spot rate** at which the trade was executed.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LastForwardPoints
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **forward points** used to derive the forward FX rate at which the trade was executed.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in Execution Reports (MsgType='8').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocLinkID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A unique ID used to **link related Allocation Instruction messages** together (e.g., linking a 'New' allocation to its 'Cancel' or 'Replace').
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This ID links a chain of allocation messages pertaining to the same underlying trades.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocLinkType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **relationship between linked allocation messages** (as identified by AllocLinkID, 196).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '0' (FX Trade), '1' (Transfer).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecondaryOrderID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **second unique order identifier** assigned by the exchange or an intermediary system. Used for systems that maintain multiple parallel internal identifiers.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Often used to reference the ID assigned by the venue, separate from the firm's OrderID (37).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoIOIQualifiers
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of IOI qualifiers** (IOIQualifier, 104) included in the message. This marks the beginning of the repeating group for IOI qualifications.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used when multiple qualifications apply to a single Indication of Interest (MsgType='6').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MaturityMonthYear
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **month and year of maturity** for a fixed income security, option, or future. Format is crucial for derivatives identification.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMM** or **YYYYMMDD**. Mandatory for futures and options.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: PutOrCall
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies whether a derivative instrument (e.g., an option) is a **Put option or a Call option**. This is mandatory for options trading and helps define the rights conveyed to the holder.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values are '0' (Put) or '1' (Call). This field is critical for correct security identification, especially when used with StrikePrice (202).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StrikePrice
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **price at which the holder of an option can buy (Call) or sell (Put)** the underlying security. This is the exercise price of the contract.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: A decimal quantity. Mandatory for options. The currency of the strike price is specified by StrikeCurrency (947).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CoveredOrUncovered
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Indicates whether the option trade is **covered by the underlying asset (covered) or not (uncovered)**. This has implications for margin and risk calculations.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values typically include '0' (Covered) or '1' (Uncovered/Naked). Used primarily for options.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CustomerOrFirm
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Indicates whether the transaction is for a **customer or for the firm's own account**. This field is primarily for regulatory and internal reporting classification.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This is now generally replaced by the OrderCapacity (528) and CustOrderCapacity (582) combination in newer FIX versions.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MaturityDay
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **day of the month** on which a security, contract, or option matures. Used in combination with MaturityMonthYear (200) for full maturity definition (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Full maturity date should be sent via MaturityMonthYear (200) or MaturityDate (541).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: OptAttribute
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A character field providing **additional attribute detail for an option**, such as its style (e.g., American vs. European).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Often used for custom option attributes or non-standard contract variations.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SecurityExchange
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **exchange or market code** where the security is listed or primarily traded. This helps ensure correct identification when the Symbol (55) is ambiguous.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in conjunction with Symbol (55) and SecurityID (48). It often contains a MIC (Market Identifier Code).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NotifyBrokerOfCredit
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A boolean indicator specifying whether the **BrokerOfCredit (92) should be notified** of the trade or allocation, usually for commission/fee purposes.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values 'Y' (Yes) or 'N' (No). BrokerOfCredit (92) itself is deprecated/replaced, but the concept of notification remains.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: AllocHandlInst
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies **how the receiving system should handle the Allocation Instruction**. This is related to the timing and processing logic of the allocation request.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '1' (Process normally), '2' (Forward to clearing firm), '3' (Forward to custodian).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: MaxShow
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **maximum quantity** (shares/contracts) of an order that should be displayed to the marketplace at any time. Used for 'Iceberg' orders (Replaced by MaxFloor, 111).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: MaxFloor (111) is typically preferred, but MaxShow is used in some versions for the same purpose.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: PegOffsetValue
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **numeric offset value** used in calculating the final price for a pegged order (OrdType='P'). This value is added to or subtracted from the peg reference price (e.g., Mid-point, Bid, Offer).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. The direction of the offset (add/subtract) is often derived from the order Side (54) or PegOffsetType (836).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: XmlDataLen
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **length in bytes** of the following XmlData (213) field. Used for transporting and delineating blocks of XML content within a FIX message.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Mandatory if XmlData (213) is present. Used for carriage of proprietary or complex, structured data.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: XmlData
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A block of **XML-formatted data** that can contain structured information not defined by standard FIX fields. The length must be specified by XmlDataLen (212).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: The MessageEncoding (347) must be consistent with the XML content encoding.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: SettlInstRefID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **reference ID** to a set of standing settlement instructions (SSI) held by a counterparty or a central matching utility.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: This ID allows a counterparty to retrieve a predefined set of settlement details rather than sending them inline.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoRoutingIDs
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of routing identifiers** (RoutingID, 217) and their types (RoutingType, 216) included in the message. Marks the beginning of a repeating group for routing instructions.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used to specify a chain of intermediaries or destinations for an order or message.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RoutingType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **type of routing identification** provided in the paired RoutingID (217). Clarifies the nature of the entity being routed to.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values include '1' (Target for the order), '2' (Target for the execution report).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RoutingID
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **actual identifier** of the entity in the routing chain, such as a broker or trading venue ID, based on the corresponding RoutingType (216).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoRoutingIDs (215).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Spread
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The difference between the BidPx (132) and OfferPx (133), or the **spread value relative to a benchmark** for a fixed income security or swap.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. For pegged orders, PegOffsetValue (211) is used instead.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Benchmark
FIX_Tags_Description.description: Specifies the **benchmark curve or index** used as a reference point for calculating prices or spreads (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Replaced by BenchmarkCurveName (221) and related fields to provide a more specific description of the benchmark.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BenchmarkCurveCurrency
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **currency** of the benchmark curve being referenced in the definition of a fixed income security.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Uses standard ISO 4217 currency codes (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BenchmarkCurveName
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **name of the underlying financial index or curve** used as the pricing reference for the instrument (e.g., LIBOR, T-Bill, Treasury).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used primarily in fixed income instruments to clarify pricing context.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: BenchmarkCurvePoint
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **point on the benchmark curve** that is relevant to the instrument's pricing (e.g., '3Y' for 3-year point, '5Y' for 5-year point).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in conjunction with BenchmarkCurveName (221).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CouponRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **interest rate (as a percentage)** paid periodically by the issuer of a fixed income security, convertible, or derivative. This defines the rate of the coupon.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity (e.g., 0.05 for 5%). Mandatory for coupon-bearing instruments.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: CouponPaymentDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date of the next scheduled interest (coupon) payment** for a debt instrument.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**. Used in security definition messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: IssueDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date the security was first issued** to the public or private market. Used as a key identifier for fixed income instruments.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**. Used in security definition messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RepurchaseTerm
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The term of a repurchase agreement (repo) in number of days (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Replaced by the general framework of DeliveryType (919) and related date fields.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RepurchaseRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The rate for a repurchase agreement (repo) (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Replaced by the general framework of DeliveryType (919) and related rate/price fields.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Factor
FIX_Tags_Description.description: A **decimal value representing the remaining principal amount** of an amortizing security (e.g., mortgage-backed securities). Used to calculate the current value.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity (e.g., 0.985). This value is multiplied by the original face value to get the current par value.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TradeOriginationDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The original **date a trade was executed or agreed upon**, typically used when reporting a trade many days after its execution date (TradeDate, 75).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**. Used in Trade Capture Reports (MsgType='AE') for delayed reporting scenarios.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ExDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **date on which a security begins trading ex-dividend or ex-rights**. This is crucial for determining which party is entitled to the dividend/right.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: ContractMultiplier
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of units of the underlying asset** represented by one derivative contract (e.g., futures or options). This is used to calculate the notional value.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Mandatory for derivatives. For example, a futures contract may have a multiplier of 1000.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: NoStipulations
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **number of specific characteristics or conditions (stipulations)** attached to a bond or fixed-income instrument. Marks the start of a repeating group.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the definition of fixed income instruments.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StipulationType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **type of stipulation** being specified for a fixed-income instrument (e.g., Maturity, Callable, Puttable).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoStipulations (232).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: StipulationValue
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **value or description** associated with the preceding StipulationType (233) (e.g., a specific date, a percentage, or a text description).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Used in the repeating group defined by NoStipulations (232).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: YieldType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **method or convention used to calculate the yield** of a bond or money market instrument (e.g., True Yield, Current Yield, Yield to Maturity).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Values are coded and determine how the accompanying Yield (236) should be interpreted.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Yield
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **calculated annual rate of return** on an investment, as determined by the YieldType (235).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Represents the actual yield value (e.g., 0.045 for 4.5%).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: TotalTakedown
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **total concession or discount** given on a bond offering from the public re-offering price to all participants in the selling group, prior to any management or underwriting fees.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in New Order List (MsgType='E') for syndication.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: Concession
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The **amount of the discount/fee** allowed to a selling group member for a bond offering. This is typically subtracted from the Total Takedown (237).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity. Used in New Order List (MsgType='E') for syndication.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RepoCollateralSecurityType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The type of collateral used in a repurchase agreement (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Replaced by the collateral structure within Collateral messages.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: RedemptionDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The date the security can be redeemed (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used. Use EventType (865)='20' (Redemption) and EventDate (866).

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingCouponPaymentDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The coupon payment date for the **underlying security** of a derivative.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingIssueDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The issue date for the **underlying security** of a derivative.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: **Format:** **YYYYMMDD**.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingRepoCollateralSecurityType
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The type of collateral used for the **underlying security** in a repurchase agreement (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingRepurchaseTerm
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The repurchase term for the **underlying security** (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingRepurchaseRate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The repurchase rate for the **underlying security** (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingFactor
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The amortization **factor** for the **underlying security**.
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Decimal quantity.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: UnderlyingRedemptionDate
FIX_Tags_Description.description: The redemption date for the **underlying security** (Deprecated).
FIX_Tags_Description.note: Deprecated/No Longer Used.

FIX_Tags_Description.name: LegCouponPaymentDate
